hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current candidates are structurally poor (pLDDT=0.0) and show marginal selectivity. The next iteration should prioritize structural quality and refine selectivity by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To filter out candidates with overly strong ddG that may be associated with off-target interactions (e.g., SSTR3/SSTR5) while still maintaining SSTR2 binding.

These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions (especially SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14 pharmacophore.
